title: aconitum × paniculatum subsp. lasiocarpum
intro: Aconitum × paniculatum subsp. lasiocarpum is a subspecies of the Aconitum × paniculatum hybrid plant, commonly known as monk's hood or wolf's bane, which is a perennial flowering plant native to East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, and is believed to possess medicinal properties due to the presence of aconitine and other alkaloids. The health benefits of the plant are said to include relief from pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Aconitum × paniculatum subsp. lasiocarpum is traditionally prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is used in herbal medicine to treat a range of conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations.
